AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.title: HbA1c above target
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.description: Your HbA1c is 7.2% — still above the 6.5% target, but trending down from 8.1% over the past year.
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.suggestedQuestion: What can I do to bring my HbA1c below 6.5%?

AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.title: Kidney function needs monitoring
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.description: Creatinine 1.3 mg/dL (slightly elevated) and eGFR 72 mL/min (below 90 target). Gradual decline over 12 months.
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.suggestedQuestion: Should I be worried about my kidney function? What steps can I take?

AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.title: LDL cholesterol improving
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.description: LDL dropped from 145 to 128 mg/dL. Atorvastatin is working, but still above the 100 target.
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.suggestedQuestion: My LDL is 128 — how much further does it need to drop and how?

AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.title: Thyroid well controlled
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.description: TSH 3.8 mIU/L — within normal range since starting Levothyroxine. Stable for 4 months.
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.suggestedQuestion: Is my thyroid condition fully under control now?

AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.title: Vitamin D insufficient
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.description: Vitamin D 22 ng/mL — below the 30 ng/mL threshold. Supplementation recommended.
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.suggestedQuestion: How much Vitamin D should I take and for how long?

AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.title: 3 new reports uploaded
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.description: HbA1c, Lipid Panel, and Kidney Function tests from Mar 2026 are ready for analysis.
AI_CONTEXT_CARDS.suggestedQuestion: Summarize the key findings from my March 2026 lab reports
